best_for: Blog posts, news articles, editorial content — establishes headline, author, publish/modify dates, and publisher
avoid_when: A page that isn't genuinely article-shaped (a product page, a landing page) — using Article schema there misrepresents the content type
tradeoffs: Enables article-specific rich results and reinforces E-E-A-T signals (author, dates) directly in structured form — keep datePublished/dateModified genuinely accurate per `SEO034`

best_for: E-commerce product pages — price, availability, SKU, and (paired with Review/AggregateRating) rating information
avoid_when: A product page where price/availability genuinely can't be confirmed accurate at render time — inaccurate Product schema risks a manual action, see `SEO024`
tradeoffs: Enables rich results showing price/availability/rating directly in search results, a meaningful click-through-rate lever for e-commerce — must stay synced with real Shopify/inventory data (`EC024`)

option: LocalBusiness (or a more specific subtype: Restaurant, Store, etc.)
best_for: Any business with a physical location or service area — address, hours, phone, and service area information
avoid_when: A purely online business with no physical location or local service area — LocalBusiness schema doesn't apply there
tradeoffs: Powers local pack visibility and Google Business Profile consistency (NAP data) — use the most specific applicable subtype (Restaurant, MedicalBusiness, etc.) rather than the generic LocalBusiness type where one exists

best_for: A genuine list of distinct questions and answers on a page, formatted to match — not content awkwardly reformatted into Q&A shape purely to gain the schema
avoid_when: Content that isn't genuinely question-and-answer structured — retrofitting non-FAQ content into FAQPage markup to chase rich results is against Google's structured data guidelines and risks a manual action
tradeoffs: Can produce an expandable FAQ rich result directly in search — genuinely useful for support/documentation content, actively risky when used as a ranking-manipulation tactic on non-FAQ content

best_for: Step-by-step instructional content — numbered steps with clear individual actions, ideally with time/materials/tools where relevant
avoid_when: Content that describes a process narratively without genuine discrete steps — forcing narrative content into HowTo's step structure produces poor-quality markup
tradeoffs: Well-suited to tutorial/recipe/DIY content specifically — note that HowTo rich results have been scaled back in some search surfaces over time, so treat this as structural clarity value first, rich-result eligibility as a bonus

best_for: Every site's homepage or a dedicated about page — legal name, logo, social profiles, and contact information for the entity behind the site
avoid_when: A personal blog/portfolio where Person schema (below) is the more accurate entity type
tradeoffs: Establishes the entity behind the site clearly for search engines and AI systems building an understanding of who owns/publishes the content — directly supports `SEO063`'s about-page trust signal in structured form

option: WebSite (with SearchAction for sitelinks search box)
best_for: A site's homepage, especially one with meaningful internal search functionality worth surfacing directly in search results
avoid_when: Small sites with no meaningful internal search to surface
tradeoffs: Can enable a sitelinks search box directly in Google results for branded searches — a small, low-effort addition with real click-through-rate upside for sites that qualify

best_for: Personal sites, individual author bio pages, portfolio sites — establishes an individual as the entity, with credentials/social profiles
avoid_when: A business/company site where Organization is the more accurate primary entity — Person schema is typically supplementary there (on author bio pages) rather than the site's primary entity
tradeoffs: Directly supports `SEO060`/`SEO061`'s author-expertise E-E-A-T signals in structured, machine-readable form on author bio pages specifically

best_for: Content featuring genuine reviews or an aggregate rating of a product/service/business — must reflect real, verifiable ratings
avoid_when: Any use of Review/AggregateRating schema without genuine underlying reviews — this is one of the most actively enforced categories for structured-data spam actions
tradeoffs: High-value for click-through rate (star ratings visible in search results) but also one of the highest-risk schema types to misuse — never display Review/AggregateRating markup without real reviews backing it (see `SEO066`)

best_for: Pages promoting a specific event with a date, location, and (where applicable) ticketing information
avoid_when: Recurring/ongoing content with no specific date-bound event to describe
tradeoffs: Enables rich event results including date/location/ticket info directly in search — particularly valuable for local/live-event discovery

best_for: Individual job listing pages — title, description, salary range (where legally required/available), location, and employment type
avoid_when: A jobs listing/index page rather than an individual posting — JobPosting schema applies per-listing, not to an aggregate listings page
tradeoffs: Powers Google's dedicated job-search experience — a genuinely distinct, high-intent traffic source separate from standard organic results for sites that hire regularly

best_for: Any page featuring video content meant to be discoverable — includes thumbnail, duration, and upload date
avoid_when: A page where video is purely decorative/background, not primary content worth surfacing in video search results
tradeoffs: Enables video rich results and video search visibility — thumbnail and duration shown directly in results meaningfully affects click-through rate for video-forward content

best_for: App/software product pages — category, operating system, price, and rating information
avoid_when: A SaaS marketing site where the product isn't distributed as a discrete downloadable/installable application — Product schema may fit better there depending on the actual offering shape
tradeoffs: Supports app-specific rich results showing rating/price/platform directly — most directly applicable to mobile apps and desktop software with a clear install/download path

best_for: Product pages with pricing, especially with multiple price points (variants, quantity tiers) or availability windows
avoid_when: A product with a single, simple price and no variant complexity — a simpler flat price/availability declaration on the Product schema itself may suffice
tradeoffs: AggregateOffer specifically handles the common e-commerce case of a price range across variants — using the wrong nesting (a flat Offer for a variant product) produces inaccurate structured data (`SEO024`)

best_for: Any page beyond the top level of the site hierarchy — see `SEO018`'s corresponding on-page breadcrumb requirement
avoid_when: A true top-level page (homepage) with no meaningful breadcrumb trail to show
tradeoffs: Low implementation cost, consistently useful — a breadcrumb trail shown directly in search results improves the perceived context/relevance of a result before the click

best_for: Ranked or ordered list content (e.g. 'Top 10 X') where the list itself is the primary content structure
avoid_when: Content that mentions multiple items without the page actually being organized as a ranked/ordered list
tradeoffs: Reinforces the list structure for search engines processing list-format content, supporting both traditional list-format rich results and AI-summary extraction of ranked content
